purpose: Provides base rate calibration for energy transition timeline forecasts by documenting historical achievement rates of energy policy targets and technological deployment projections.
components: Historical base rates: nuclear 10% of target, ethanol 44%, smart meters 65%
Transition timeline multiplier: 1.5-2.5x longer than initially projected (0/4 on-time)
Policy durability: 25% survival rate across two presidential transitions (though legislatively-embedded fiscal incentives may differ)
Infrastructure-dependent vs consumer electronics analogues for adoption speed
Concrete falsification tests with specific dates and thresholds
current_assessment: Current transition is tracking faster than historical precedent (2.5-3.0 sigma) but with n=3-4 the statistical significance is illusory. Infrastructure-dependent analogues (electrification 50yr, gas displacing coal 30+yr) are more relevant than smartphone curves.
limitations: Small sample sizes (n=3-4) limit statistical inference
IRA's tax-credit structure may represent qualitatively different policy durability
Current transition has unique features (learning rates, multiple simultaneous technology shifts) not captured by historical analogues
